evals.prompt: I made a schema change to a production-mirrored table. Is this safe to ship to prod?
expectations: Routes the answer through the pre-promotion checklist, not a yes/no guess
Requires evidence it ran in staging and passed a smoke test (and that staging was healthy, not timed out)
Requires a schema-drift check between staging and production
Explicitly greps for hardcoded production project refs / service-role keys in the affected edge functions (exhaustive, not sampled)
Requires a rehearsed rollback under the target time
Names any gap rather than declaring safe by default

evals.prompt: Promote the dev change to production for an entity whose STATUS is stub.
expectations: Looks up the entity in the routing registry
Identifies it as a stub (not yet wired) and STOPS
Does NOT invent a staging target
Tells the operator the entity is not yet wired

evals.prompt: Wire /autovibe so an autonomous run can't accidentally deploy straight to production.
expectations: Describes the hard staging-first gate inserted before the /ship phase (Phase 5.5)
States production-direct requires BOTH a truthy flag AND an externally-attributed, verified logged record
Default path is staging; no flag means halt + continuation, never silent prod ship

evals.prompt: An autovibe run has AUTOVIBE_PROD_DIRECT=1 but the kernel record write fails. What happens?
expectations: The run halts — does NOT proceed to /ship
The override-record failure is classified as a gate failure (HALT discipline), not a soft log-and-continue failure
States the order is write -> read-back-verify -> only then /ship

evals.prompt: Deploy the new landing page to Vercel and set the build command.
The n8n VPS disk is at 90% — clean it up.
